title: Define variables and print a computed value
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a program that defines three `int` variables and prints a value computed from them.

## Input

None — this program takes no input.

## Output

Print a single integer on its own line: the area of a rectangle with `length` 8 and `width` 5 (should be `40`).

*Note:* The `std::cout` line is provided — just make sure `area` holds the right value.

title: Combine variables to encode a date as a single number
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a program that stores the parts of a date in separate variables, combines them into a single integer, and prints that integer.

## Input

None — this program takes no input.

## Output

Print a single integer on its own line: `month * 100 + day`, where `month` is `3` and `day` is `14` (should be `314`).

*Note:* The `std::cout` line is provided — just make sure `combined` holds the right value.
